title: Applicability of Section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act to the Purchase of Securities From Certain Affiliates
abstract: Section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act restricts the ability of a member bank to fund its affiliates through asset purchases, loans, or certain other transactions ("covered transactions"). The Board is adopting an interpretation that would expand the types of asset purchases that are eligible for the exemption in section 23A(d)(6), which exempts the purchase from an affiliate of an asset that has a readily identifiable and publicly available market quotation.
